title: AI and the Future of Software Engineering

statements.text: Within five years, AI will make most mid-level coding roles economically unjustifiable.
The real risk of AI in engineering is not job loss, but a collapse in deep technical judgment.
Teams that ban AI coding tools today are acting as responsibly as teams that once banned cloud.
Software quality will improve with AI, even if fewer engineers understand the code they ship.
Computer science education should reduce emphasis on syntax and focus on system verification.
Senior engineers are more threatened by AI than juniors because experience is easier to imitate than learn.
The best developers will soon be defined more by review and orchestration than by implementation.
AI will widen the gap between elite engineers and everyone else more than it replaces jobs outright.
Relying on AI for routine coding is no more harmful than relying on compilers or frameworks.
The industry is overestimating AI's impact on employment and underestimating its impact on liability.

context.topic: Ai and the future of software engineering
context.additional_context: Ai seems to take over the software engineering space. More and more engineers are worried about job safety, education, and software quality. But is this really the things to worry about.
